TLDR"
""Discussing roles and titles on general level is hard because the meaning is created on the company level. Focus on defining the role early together with your team and manager. This definition should also be revisited every time there is significant change in the team, the company or the industry.""
""
"What’s up with all the titles? "
"UX designer, UI designer, UX/UI designer, Product designer, Interaction designer, Visual designer. What is the difference? Do we need all of them? What am I? Who am I? "
""
"It is apparent that we (UX/UI/ whatever) designers are looking for an identity. But we are looking in the wrong place. A universal role definition will not tell us who we are or need to be. Because there is no ONE definition to rule them all. "
""
"I don’t mean the discussion about role definition is bad. It needs to take place, just not on the global level (or at LinkedIn). Instead you, your team and company need to define it. "
"- What titles do you have and what do they mean? "
"- What does a ___ designer do here? "
"- What are the expectations, responsibilities, skills, deliveries and so on? "
"- Where do different roles overlap and what will happen inside that overlap so you don’t step on each other toes or drop the ball?"
""
"Your title is an empty box"
"What the title contains is much more important than the title in it self. What is the difference between Junior Designer, Mid Designer, Senior Design and Design Lead? If there is no clear difference in responsibilities, skill level, experience and mandate then the distinction is totally meaningless."
""
"Titles are relative"
"Imagine that you are one of few designers at a tech company. Your design skills and experience is way greater than your fellow developers. You are the design authority and could easily call yourself senior or lead designer without any objections. "
"Now you start at a design company where everyone is dedicated designer. Suddenly you are not the expert, you may hardly pass as mid level. It can feel like you have been demoted, but you have just switch the eco system and both you and your title need to adapt. "
""
"Of course the you can go in the opposite direction. I’m Head of Design at a little startup. I'm the most experienced designer by far in that group. That doesn’t mean I can be Head of Design at Figma or Slack. I would get crushed in an instance by the responsibility or the imposter syndrome. The title is just an function in its own environment."
""
"Don’t let your title define you, be the one that defines your title. "
"Mic drop. 🎙

Perfection is the golden treasure at the end of the rainbow. If we just work hard and long enough, we will reach it. But that’s not how things work. Perfection is unnatural. Perfection is the end state where nothing can be added or removed. "
""
"Life is the opposite. Life is always moving, evolving and trying out new things. There is no end state. Just the best adaptation to the current state. Then things change and new adaptations are necessary."
""
"Striving for perfection is like chasing a ghost. Being perfectionist is not a strength, it’s a liability. You will never be satisfied and only focus on the shortcomings. Perfectionism is contra productive. It leads to wasted resources and burnouts. Practice makes you better, not perfect. "
""
"Instead strive for excellence. It’s at much broader goal, easier to reach and measure. You can be excellent in many different ways, so it is also more inclusive. No one is, can or want to be perfect. So let us scratch that word from our vocabulary. "
""
"THAT would be perfect.  "
""
"Have an excellent weekend everyone  😎"
""

We designers talk, do, think, breathe and live design. It’s our northern star, our guiding light. We value things by how well designed they are. We spend lot of time studying design and design history, discussing similarities and differences across disciplines, pro’s and cons with various methods, what design is good and what is bad. "
""
"We get genuinely surprised when the rest of the population don’t share the same passion, interest and devotion to design as we do. For us design is the answer to all questions. Others associate design with nice packaging or the distant part of the organisation that don’t create any real value. "
""
"When we don’t get the attention and appreciation we deserve we feel misunderstood and unimportant. Because for us design is everything. But guess what. For everyone else it's not. "
""
"Design is a vital part of the product, the process and the organisation. Take it away and you get a grey, boring tech company that nobody cares about. "
""
"So we make a difference. We are an important part of a whole. A system within a bigger system. The question is not about how we can get a seat at the table or gaining more power or louder voice. It is not about us versus them and who is driving or deciding. "
""
"The question is about how we can create great synergies with other parts of the system and together build even better products, organisations and companies. "

🌉 UX is a bridge between business and the customer."
""
"UX is not a bottle of ketchup, it’s a bridge. It transforms the abstract ideas and strategies of the business to something tangible and concrete that a customer can interact with. In order to build a bridge you need to be familiar with both sides. "
""
"It’s easy to fall for the illusion that UX is all about costumers and user needs. Like, “it’s called user experience not business experience.” From company side those are two different areas that seldom meet. But users and customers don't care about your silos. They see the products as one thing. Pricing, support, tech, look and feel, it's all merged down to one experience.  "
""
"That's why you should care about stuff outside your department. Things like product strategy, roadmap, pricing, marketing, business plans, churn rate and so on. 🌉 UX is a bridge between business and the customer."
""
"UX is not a bottle of ketchup, it’s a bridge. It transforms the abstract ideas and strategies of the business to something tangible and concrete that a customer can interact with. In order to build a bridge you need to be familiar with both sides. "
""
"It’s easy to fall for the illusion that UX is all about costumers and user needs. Like, “it’s called user experience not business experience.” From company side those are two different areas that seldom meet. But users and customers don't care about your silos. They see the products as one thing. Pricing, support, tech, look and feel, it's all merged down to one experience.  "
""
"That's why you should care about stuff outside your department. Things like product strategy, roadmap, pricing, marketing, business plans, churn rate and so on. Expand your UX curiosity and knowledge towards your own company, product and business. The more you know and understand about both sides, the stronger bridge you can build. So let’s start building. 🏗 "
""

"Many thinkers and modern philosophers have struggled with this complicated relationship between production and consumption."
" "
"👉 Who is going to consume all the stuff we are (over)producing?"
"👉  Why are we making (and buying) so much more than we actually need? "
"👉  What is going to happen when AI will make the production line spin faster and faster? "
""
"The long tail was supposed to give us choice and fill needs outside the mainstream. Instead we will be drowned in a tsunami of digital overproduction. It will be hard to know what information can be trusted, what is authentic (human made) and what is just an AI remix. "
""
"When cost of production goes down, cost of attention goes up. A never ending abundance of everything will make it enven harder to make choices that keep us happy and satisfied. There will be always something else, something better. If not now, so tomorrow. We will need AI to sort through all the staff other AI:s are churning out. An AI powered AI filter. Instead of being empowered, we will outsource even more decision making.  "
""
"🤖 What if we instead used AI to produce less but smarter? Products that last longer, cost less energi, time and material to produce? New materials and products that can easily be repaired, refurbished and recycled. That can be used more often and by more and last longer. "
" "
"We don’t need more stuff. We don’t need to 10x our efficiency and productivity. We need better products and make it easier for ordinary people to make good choices. "
""
"#ai #future #empower #sustainability

Life will find a way. So will users. If your product don't fulfil users needs they will tweak it, brake it, pick it apart and put it together again and if nothing works, find another product. "
""
"This is actually a good thing. Users are engaged, and resourceful. If they are using your product in unexpected ways, they are showing you their unfulfilled needs. "
""
"If it is a behaviour aligned with your product, incorporated it in the product. If it as un undesired behaviour, find ways to steer it in desired direction without punishing or blaming the user. They are not "holding it wrong". 😅 "
"Find the underlying need and meet it with a different and more productive way. "
""
"#need #problem #userexperience

Say whaaat? What have we been doing all the time if not meeting needs and designing experiences?"
""
"No worries. We have been doing great things and helping a lot of users. We have just not been designing THE experience. Because we can't. We have no control over the users, what they think, what they do, feel or experience. Probably not even users them selves have control over their experiences and reactions. The experience happens automatically and have been happening even befor the UX field has been invented. We just started to notice that it matters, A LOT. "
""
"What is experience anyway"
"My own homebrewed definition: “Experience (the outcome) emerges when the expectations of the user meet our product (the output).”"
"Our design, the output, is the part that we can fully control and design. User expectations can be nudged or influenced, but not controlled. When the expectations meet (or collide) with our product, all the feelings, benefits, thoughts and reactions that happens are the experience. This experience could be prominent and memorable, like an excellent meal you never tasted before, or totally invisible like fresh air. "
""
"Why this is important "
"Is this just semantics or does it actually matter? I think it matters, because we might think that we can control and are supposed to design the actual experience. If the experience is not what we want, we can just tweak the design like a knob to get the right result. "
""
"But the user experience is not a predictable Pavlovian reflex. Every user is unique and so is their experience. What we are doing is designing by proxy. We as designers need to be aware of the gap between what we can and can not control. We design one product and aim for a certain output but create countless unique experiences. Some wanted, some not, some good and unfortunately some bad. "
""
"So what’s next"
"- Users expectations are just as important os your design for the reaching the desired outcome."
"- People are entitled to their experience and opinion. It doesn’t mean that they are right or that you need to do anything about it. "
"- There will always be a gap between your intent and the result. This gap is your greatest source for future improvements"
"- And finally but most importantly. It is important that we are aware and feel responsible for all the different experiences that our product creates. Act if your product or design causes harm, even if it’s unintended and could not be predicted or prevented. "
""
"#uxdesign #ux #lie #experience #responsibility

I spend a lot of time researching, thinking, creating concepts and prototypes, testing and tweaking. It’s a lot of work and it feels important. My employee pays me good money and gives me nice feedback. All good right?"
""
"But one painful insight hit me like a brick. It doesn’t matter how many cool concepts or prototypes I create. If they don’t end up in the hands of users they are just pretty pictures. The users don’t care about my design if it’s never realised, developed and released. In other words, my design by it self is worthless. My job should not end with delivering a fancy Figma file. It is fulfils its goal when the users get the promised benefit and the targeted needs are met. "
""
"This doesn’t mean that we can skip the design part and just focus on delivering stuff. Many have tried, few (products) survived. Design and the discovery part is vital. It’s the compass pointing out the right direction before you start running. It doesn't matter how fast you run if you are on a wrong path. Purposeful design 10x your chances of success. "
""
"Design without execution is nothing. "
"Design with great executions is everything. "
""
"#design #purpose #impact

So many things in our lives are running on autopilot. Especially those seemingly mundane task lite making breakfast, commute to work, doing laundry, going to the gym, paying bills. We often zoom out and just wish that we could be done already so we can move on to something more fun. "
""
"That should be great right? Less of those boring stuff and more time for fun. "
"But what if are we giving up when everything is easy and frictionless? Are we actually spending the time we saved on something better? Or do we just do even more mundane things?"
""
"Imagine going to the gym with only super light weights or video games with Easy as the only option. You don’t need to read long books or articles, everything is already curated and summarised. The recommendation algorithms adjust exactly to your preferences and even the content is custom made just for you. You don’t longer have to spend years practicing writing or painting, just some good prompts and click a button. "
""
"If designers job is to remove friction and make life easier, this should be the best case scenario. Why does it not “feel” right?"
""
"Because we don’t want to remove all friction and effort. Just remove it where it should not be and keep it where we need it and want it. We grow from overcoming challenges, we learn from mistakes, we get insights from thinking and reflecting, we need scarcity to boost our creativity. We want to slow down before making a big decision or where things get dangerous. Our bodies and minds are not built for abundance. If we get everything we want, we actually get depressed. "
""
"In other words, we are built to solve problems. We just need to make sure we are solving the right ones. "
""
"Inspired by Per Axbom: When friction is a good thing"
"https://lnkd.in/d3-KZFcM"
""
"#frictionless #friction #thinking #growthmindset

👿 Better the devil you know. "
"Or why individuals don’t want to deal with change."
"As designer I want to create new and better ways for doing things. My hope is to improve peoples lives, make them happier, more efficient or productive. But there is an invisible opposite force that is often forgotten. The unwillingness to change. "
""
"🛑 Change is hard and risky"
"Even if the current situation is far from perfect, many prefer the familiarity and that feels safe. Better the devil you know, as the saying goes. Finding and learning new ways takes time and energi. Time that we don’t have and energi we don’t want to waste. It is hard to know of the effort is worth it. There is also a risk of making a bad choice and ending up with something even worse. "
""
"🥇Low risk and high reward "
"To help people to take this leap of fate we need to minimise the threshold and maximise the benefit. Showing how fast, safe and easy it is to change to the new system. No data is lost, all previous settings are saved, no hard decisions are required during the instalment. As much as possible should happen automatically or taken care of by external experts. The benefits to the end user should be clear, tangible and imminent. "
""
"🙂 Design the default "
"Why should designers think about the default setting if the users can change it when ever they want? The truth is that the majority of users will never or seldom make changes that are not required. Especially if the consequences of the other options are not obvious. Changes always include risk. "
"That’s why we must design the default settings to be perfectly adjusted for beginners and suitable for the most common tasks. More advanced features should be introduced later, when users are comfortable with the basic functions. "
""
"#change #fear #devil #default

Where does our intuition comes from and should we always trust it?"
" "
"Intuition comes from long experience from a specific field, with lot of practice and feedback. A skilled basketball player knows if a throw will hit or miss even before the ball reaches its target. "
""
"So yes, if you are making a decision within your field of expertise you can trust your intuition. It is actually your aggregated knowledge that is giving you the answer. "
""
"BUT for decisions we seldom make, when we lack the experience or where feedback is limited the intuition is not so helpful. When buying your first car or a house it should not just “feel” good or “seem” nice. "
""
"Another example when intuition gets in the way is in the hiring process. People prefer what they know and avoid what they don’t know. A hiring manger can “instinctively” prefer a candidate that is similar to them, from the  "
"same gender, social group, ethnicity, school or some other shared factor. "
"Here is important to analyse WHY someone seems to be a good fit. Does it just “feel right” or is it also the best candidate objectively. "
""
"This is just one of many insights from Dan Ariely’s short LinkedIn course on how to make good decisions."
"https://lnkd.in/dkYtgfHt"
""
"#decisionmaking #bias #intuition #hiring

Why is it so much easier to give advice to others but so hard to giver it to our selves? It should be the opposite, nobody knows ours selves better than we do, what we want, like and need. "
""
"Maybe that is the problem. We know too much and it makes it hard to think. Logic and emotion gets mixed up, the line between what we want to do and should do gets blurry. That’s when an objective point of view can be helpful. You can actually do it on your own with a bit of imagination. Here are two ways: "
""
"😇 What would Jesus do?"
"Invite an imaginary advisor that you admire or that is good at handle similar situations. What would that person say or do? How would their solution work for you? "
""
"❓ Asking for a friend "
"Take yourself out of the equation and make a distance between you and the problem. Now you can look at the problem objectively without your emotional bagage being in the way. What would your best advice be to your “friend”? What are the pros and cons?"
""
"You can try both ways and write down the options. Hopefully you now have at least one good way forward. If not, try talking to a real friend. 😅

Why do we lose our curiosity when we grow up?"
"Is it the school that teaches us all the old knowledge and discourage us from finding new one? "
"Are the social norms holding us back, where it’s safer to stay quiet rather than ask and be seen as ignorant? "
"Or is it we and our existing knowledge that are blocking new experiences?"
""
"Maybe it’s the combination of all but the result is still the same. We stop being curious and challenge the obvious. "
"- Why is the sky blue? "
"- It just is, it has always been, no point in asking. "
""
"We must learn how to look beyond the obvious, challenge the status quo and start feeling the joy of not knowing and finding out. If your company or a product was a set of Lego and you could pick it appart. What else could you build? How would it look like if it started from scratch today? "
"Silly question but it makes you think. Doesn’t it. "
""
"- So start with Why."
"- Why?"
"- Perfect. "
""

I’m really active when I talk, not so much when listening. To fix that, I came up with this routine. I go through it before every planed conversation. 😅  "
""
"👂Listen"
"Be quiet just listen to what the other person is saying. Don’t think about what to say next, assume, presume, interrupt or multitask. Just be. "
""
"🤔 Think"
" Write down any important thoughts that pop up. Important is the key word. If you don’t have an answer don’t be afraid to ask for a moment. ”What a good question, let me think about it for a bit.” "
""
"💬 Talk"
"When you have an answer in mind, find a brief and clear way to communicate it. Say it and then stop talking and wait for reaction. Enjoy the silence. It is your friend, not a hole that needs to be filled. "
""
"#activelistening #silence #kahneman #thinkfastandslow #listening

Don’t give them what they want"

As a child, it’s your job to listen and learn from the elders. This is a logical order of things, the old want to help the younglings avoiding known mistakes and even to stay alive. "Watch out for the tiger bi the river or the poisonous berries." "
""
"But don’t let this be your only truth. Learn from mistakes of others but don't forget to make your own unique mistakes. You don’t need a lot of experience, knowledge or a degree to be a teacher. Neither should you stop learning just because you have reached certain title, age or your imagined potential. Learning is growth and potential for growth is limitless. "
""
"Teach: find something you are good at or interested in. It can actually be something that you yourself want to learn. Teaching it to someone else will help you to understand it much deeply and also remember what you learned. "
""
"Learn: Don’t fall into the expert trap, or thinking that you have seen it all. Find new ways to look at the old problems, or find new problems to solve. Don’t see young talents as threat, but as opportunity to learn new skills. Let your kid teach you how to play Fortnite or Roblox. Sharing their world will broaden yours. "
""
"Have fun: Learning and teaching done in a good way is really rewarding and fun. Most schools have just not figured out how."
""
"#experience #job #learning #teaching #growth

A certain amount of unemployment is actually desired for the financial system to work. It is called “unemployment balance” and is supposed to be around 2%, just like the inflation. This is not my area of expertise so I will not dive in any deeper. Let me know if I’m way off track. "
""
"The interesting part is that unemployment is not a bug, it’s a feature designed by the system. Other systems like communism have tried to remove the unemployment with planed economy but failed miserably. So we are stuck with the market based financial system. It thrives when there is certain imbalance and tension, where all the needs are not satisfied, where we want yet another product to make our lives even easier. Chasing the fairytale of infinite growth and heavenly comfort. "
""
"In good times, companies need a lot of new workers to meet the growing demand. In bad times, the workers are just dead weight. We can, and probably should, demand more responsibility from companies and measure their success with other metrics than just profit. Creating jobs or healing the planet for example. But companies are also caught in the system and must obey the rules in order to survive. Firing employees instead of going under is their natural defence mechanism. "
""
"My point is this: You getting fired is not on you. You have done nothing wrong, and are not to blame. You are actually making the system work by making your services available to other companies. Without you they are not going to be able to grow. You are important.🏅 "
""
"#work #growth #success #unemployment  #economy #unemployed #jobs #system #finance

A certain amount of unemployment is actually desired for the financial system to work. It is called “unemployment balance” and is supposed to be around 2%, just like the inflation. This is not my area of expertise so I will not dive in any deeper. Let me know if I’m way off track. "
""
"The interesting part is that unemployment is not a bug, it’s a feature designed by the system. Other systems like communism have tried to remove the unemployment with planed economy but failed miserably. So we are stuck with the market based financial system. It thrives when there is certain imbalance and tension, where all the needs are not satisfied, where we want yet another product to make our lives even easier. Chasing the fairytale of infinite growth and heavenly comfort. "
""
"In good times, companies need a lot of new workers to meet the growing demand. In bad times, the workers are just dead weight. We can, and probably should, demand more responsibility from companies and measure their success with other metrics than just profit. Creating jobs or healing the planet for example. But companies are also caught in the system and must obey the rules in order to survive. Firing employees instead of going under is their natural defence mechanism. "
""
"My point is this: You getting fired is not on you. You have done nothing wrong, and are not to blame. You are actually making the system work by making your services available to other companies. Without you they are not going to be able to grow. You are important.🏅 "
""
"#work #growth #success #unemployment  #economy #unemployed #jobs #system #finance

Are you looking for A job or THE job?"
"Although sounding similar those are two totally different things, process and mindsets. There is also different amount of urgency and motivation. Before staring a new process you should know where you at and what you are looking for."
""
"A Job"
"This is just a job like any other. The one you take when you want to save up some money, pay the bills or just do something while you figure out the future. You know (or hope) that this is not what you will be doing for the rest of your life. Don’t apply jobs you already know that you will hate. If being a telemarketer is not you thing, don’t do it. "
""
"Even if this is not the job of your dreams, there is always something you can learn. Are you an aspiring UX designer, but work at McDonalds? Here is a great opportunity to observe. What are people buying, when and how? Talk to people, make friends, learn how things work. "
""
"THE Job"
"This is the job you actually want. May it be the job of your dreams or the job that will take you closer to your goal. My advice is to not apply for THE job when you really need A job. When the urgency is high, the desperation starts showing. When you are desperate, you have no leverage in the conversation. You will just take the first best thing. That’s why it’s a lot easier to look for a new job while still being employed, or at least have a stable financial situation. Both you and the company have other options, there is a balance in the conversation and negotiation. Both can make an unforced, conscious decision. "
""
"Find max five companies that you really want to work. Do your research and answer the key question “Why you and Why them”. Your answers should be similar but different for every company. Now you ready to start the process. If nothing works out, find five new ones. God speed."
"#ux #opportunity #motivation #people #application

Lets start with some definitions: "
""
"What is an idea?"
"Ideas are like a piece of a puzzle. If the piece doesn't fit, it doesn't mean that the piece is bad. It is just in the wrong context. If you see it like this, there are no bad ideas. While brainstorming, you are actually filling your box with different pieces of the puzzle. No matter when in the process an idea pops up, if you can't or don't want to use it, save it for later."
""
"What is a bad idea?"
"Just as with the puzzle pieces, to evaluate an idea you need to place it in the right context. Does it fit with other already established pieces? If yes, find a way to test the idea and see if it holds water. If it's a match, you have a good idea with high probability of success. Pursuing an idea just because it seems easy, cool or is suggested by a HIPPO without any proof is a high risk. Success is left to chance."
""
"The conclusion"
"The value of an idea is proven only AFTER it is applied. It is hard to know beforehand if the idea will be successful or not. With experience, research and testing you will make your guesses more precise.  "
"In other words: Using an idea without testing it first is a bad idea."
""
"#ideas #validation  #testing #research #experience #success

Much has been said bout feedback but one thing that is seldom mentioned in the same context is hierarchy. In other words WHO is giving the feedback and why. When you get feedback from your peers it is one thing. You can consider it as a friendly advice that you can choose to follow or not."
""
"When the feedback comes from your manger, stakeholder or client suddenly the situation gets more complicated. The hierarchy makes feedback more unclear. Is it still and advice that you can disregard or are you expected to follow through on everything that has been said? Are you free to question the feedback or choose to apply certain parts? Hard to know."
""
"To avoid misunderstandings, you need to establish what kind of feedback is given. There are three types of feedback:  "
""
"1. Direction"
"This are changes that your manager expects you to do. Here is no need or room for long discussions. Just make sure that you understand what, where, why. "
""
"2. Suggestion "
"Changes that your manager wants you to consider but leaves up to you if and how to apply. Make sure to understand what your manager is after, what is the reason for that suggestion. Maybe you will find other ways to reach that result. "
""
"3. Idea"
"This is exactly what sounds like, an idea. Just because it comes from your manager doesn’t mean it is good or worth more than yours. Write them down to show that you are taking them seriously but it's up to you to use them or not. "
""
"It is important that both you and your manager are aligned on what type of feedback is given. A feedback session with several topics should start with type one, the most crucial and important changes. Than move on to type two and round up with type three ideation."
""
"#feedback #hierarchy #manager #design

This main difference in needs between roles is often overlooked. We think that others need what we need and want what we want. We designers love problems, by solving them we create value for the customers. We want to share and discuss the problems to generate more ideas. "
""
"Unfortunately most managers don’t have time for our problems and don’t want to make even more decisions. We designers need to get better at packaging the problems, present alternatives, different outcomes and have a preferred best option. If it’s supported with test and data it’s even better. Treat the needs of your manager with the same care and respect as your customers. "
""
"Managers on other hand want to get to the results as fast as possible, sometimes bypassing all the time consuming parts and jump directly to conclusions. The result is a readymade list of features that the customers say they want. It not just robs you of a big and important part of your job. It’s also a trap. If you don’t know what problem you are solving and why you will just end up with a bunch of features. Try to convince your boss to let you backtrack the “solutions” to form a hypothesis and test it, with minimum time and effort. If it works, complement your manager on being right. If it doesn’t, let the manager know that you just saved a bunch of money. They (and you) win in both cases. 😍 "
""
"Read more: Escaping the Build Trap by Melissa Perri"
"#designers #manager #buildtrap #design #testing

Just to make it clear, I think you are great. Every time I visit the IKEA warehouse I’m surprised by something new, both the furniture, how the store is organised or how the checkout functions. The innovation is always present, you are not just keeping up with the current trends, you are shaping the future of Swedish households."
"BUT…, where there is a front there is always a but. There is one big area were no innovation has occurred for a long, long time. It is the last mile delivery. Maybe it works great for the majority, but my experience made me reconsider our relationship. A quick recap: "
""
"I booked an assisted delivery to our office. The only option is an unspecified four hour delivery window. The driver made one quick call, that I of course managed to miss, and moved on. No second call, no way to contact the driver. Feedback from IKEA support was that I didn’t complete my part of the agreement. I could book and pay for a new delivery or cancel the order. The end. "
""
"If this happened ten-twenty year ago, I wouldn’t react so strongly. It was how things used to work back then in the dark ages. A lot has happened since then. Services like Uber, Budbee and Mathem that don’t lay all the responsibility and work on me as a customer. Instead, they give me a sense of control and transparency, I can track everything, see the truck on a map and get constant updates. If something goes wrong, it's on them to fix that. I feel seen, heard, understood and safe. I feel trust. "
""
"It is time for you IKEA, dear old fiend, to take this seriously. As a customer I don’t care that the delivery is handled by a third party. You chose them, not me. This makes my experience and my trust in you suffer. I know that you can do better. With your size and innovation power I expect nothing less. Help me to make our relationship great again. "
""
"But first you have to deliver. Literally."
""
"#innovation #ikea #rage #experience #servicedesign #future #furniture

In my early carrier I had hard time understanding how the business part of the company related to my work as a designer, or how my design impacted the business. For me, business and design were opposites, one run by numbers and the other fuelled by creativity. The first took money from customers, while the other produced value for the users. "
""
"I saw myself as some kind of Robin Hood of UX, protecting the innocent users from the greed of business people, only chasing profit and growth. When they pushed, I needed to push back. This created much frustration and unnecessary tension, especially when it was a battle I could not win. The house always wins. "
""
"It was my transition to Product design that widened my perspective. What used to be a single user flow mow became an ecosystem, where the product and the user empowered each other. The product generated value that was consumed by the user, which gave the company money that were consumed and transformed to new value. A circular win-win machine."
""
"I started to see business, design and tech as the three main components of a product. They may vary in size or emphasis in different companies but the y are always present and interacting. This perspective removed the tension and replaced it with harmony. Design needs business just as much as business needs design. "
""
"There is still much work to be done to remove the silos and mistrust between the disciplines. We designers can contribute with what we know best, to understand the needs of others and design ways to bridge those gaps. "
""
"#design #business #productdesign #productdesign #growth #colaboration #silos"
"

We designers take pride in our work, we feel that is representing us, our skills and values. The goal of the design is to get it approved, implemented and shipped with as few deviations from our original sketches as possible, trying to make it through all the obstacles of design reviews, user testing and requests from stakeholders. "
""
"This is at least how I used to think and truth be told, it didn’t work out so well. This is a “me against the world” perspective, either you are with me, or against me. My design either survives or dies by the hands of managers and stakeholders. I also saw myself as the expert on a mission to educate and enlighten the masses. I just needed to make the nonbelievers to see the light.  "
""
"But what if there is another way? What if your design is not the main actor but a way to communicate information? What if you stop selling and pitching your design to managers, stakeholders and clients and instead started treating them as co-creators or co-enablers? What if you the goal is not to realise YOUR design but the BEST solution for the user? It does not mean that you should let go of all your ideas and believes. Just make them part of the result not THE result. "
""
"If nothing else helps, remember this: The harsh truth is that your bosses, your company or your clients actually own your design. If you get paid, your design already belongs to someone else. "
"#design #work #stakeholders #perspective

""
"This is my favourite quote of all times, that says so much in just a few words. Just change the wording a more gender neutral form and you get an universal solution to the majority of all problems. "
""
"Why is it so powerful? Because it tells me that my own thoughts and opinions are not the truth. That my likes and dislikes are mostly based on familiarity. We usually like what we know and dislike or fear what we don’t know. It tells us that the key to insights and understanding is through curiosity. We don’t even have to agree with the other person, or make them change their mind. We just need to listen and relate."
""
"Imagine how different the world would look like if we applied this way of thinking. So many conflicts and wars that could be avoided. The Avengers would be a total different movie if they all sat down with Thanos and heard his version. “Good idea, but we could probably brainstorm alternative the ways to get there. Instead of decimating the population in a snap, we could just double the resources. What do you say, big guy?""

"Why is it so powerful? Because it tells me that my own thoughts and opinions are not the truth. That my likes and dislikes are mostly based on familiarity. We usually like what we know and dislike or fear what we don’t know. It tells us that the key to insights and understanding is through curiosity. We don’t even have to agree with the other person, or make them change their mind. We just need to listen and relate."
""

""
"SNAP"
""

I used to work with a product where my main purpose was to design / produce new functionality. And that’s what we designers do. Design new stuff. But there was something missing. A certain “Groundhog Day” feeling started to emerge. Every new feature was like starting over, like adding another brick to a wall. Or was it a building. Or a bridge? Nobody new. "
""
"You now the story about two bricklayers. When asked what they are doing the first one responded “I’m a bricklayer, I’m laying bricks to feed my family”. The other one responded, “I’m a cathedral builder. I’m building a great cathedral to The Almighty.”"
""
"This is often what is missing, a vision that paint a bigger picture and inspires. Even the most mundane task becomes suddenly important. It’s not just a brick, it’s a vital part of beautiful cathedral. "
"#design #vision #motivation

I have never understood the phrase of work life balance. I’m makes me think of Severance, where those two are completely different things. ;-) But in real life they are not. The work is just a part part of your life where it needs to coexist with other parts, like your family time, your own time and sleep. If one part starts making trouble all others will follow. If you don’t sleep well, your work results will not be great for long. "
""
"If we just keep on talkin about this work / life balance, we are excluding a big group where the lack of work is actually causing a lot of stress. Suddenly one has a lot of time but it just makes everything even worse. Even our kids that don’t have a job yet, can experience a lot of pressure and stress at school. They can also be burned out and need to find a way to recharge. "
""
"It’s not the work it self, the working hours or demanding tasks, that are causing the harm. It’s the lack of control, inability to handle uncertainty or predict he future. So instead of trying to spilt the day in half and put different labels on it we should look how at the quality of life as whole, how rewarding it is and how we experience it. "
"#work #experience #wellbeing #balance

Suddenly I find myself counting fingers on every picture I’m about to like. AGI is not only threatening our jobs but the sense of reality and trust. I feel cheated. Not because the output is bad, just the opposite. It creates inner insecurity about the material I’m interacting with, its origin and intent. I feel confused, without even knowing why. Probably because my old patterns and biases suddenly don’t apply. I can’t take shortcuts. What used to take milliseconds now demand effort and risk. "
""
"We are entering a world with multiple realities without almost no borders or distinctions between them. Probably we will adapt. Just as there is no clear distinction between online and IRL, the reality and alternative reality will blend to some king of semi-reality. Both the dream and reality at the same time. Maybe my future best friend will not even exist in the real world but will be as real to me as a physical person. "
""
"Until then I’ll keep counting fingers. 😅🖐 "
""
"#jobs #future #ai #reality #agi

Everyone has an opinion about design. Nice, ugly, like it, don't like it. But going deeper than that is hard. What in the design is making you feel in a certain way? What parts are good and what parts can be improved? To translate feelings to arguments and constructive feedback is not easy. It takes training and experience. Not even designers are always good at the talking part. "
""
"Start with asking questions"
"The best way to learn about design is to talk about design. Show curiosity and that you care. Ask your local designers how and why they made certain decisions. What other options did they try? What worked, what didn’t and why? What would happen if you changed this or that?"
""
"Problem instead of solutions"
"Don’t give designers precise instruction on what to change unless you are absolutely sure that it will solve the problem. Even then you need to give reason for how you think and make room for other solutions. "
"So instead of asking designers to make the logo bigger 😉, try to articulate your concerns and ask for ways to solve them. Are you presenting your personal opinion or is it supported by data, research or opinions of others. "
""
"Need more help?"
"I will be happy to continue this conversation with you. Just reach out and we can chat. Don’t be a stranger.  "
""
"If you need help in your project with improving conversations, communication or the good old UX, I’m always here for you. Let's start a great conversation. "
"#design #ux #conversation #feedback

Our primitive mind plays trick on us and interprets mundane situations like feedback as emergencies. Unfortunately we often fall into the trap and automatically react with flee or fight action. A simple question about your design choices can feel like an attack on your personality and questioning your value as a person. "
""
"It takes effort to deliberately ignore that first instinct, to calm your racing heart and take a step back. Distance yourself from the subject that is being discussed. By taking yourself out of “your design”, it becomes just “a design” that can be viewed objectively. Now you can respond in an articulate, neutral and constructive way. This takes time and practice, but the reward is definitely worth it. "
""
"#design #react #respond #feedback

Jag går runt och funderar på hur AI kommer påverka mitt sätt att se på och utöva design. Så jag frågade ChatGPT och tycker att svaret är rätt bra.  Håller du med?"
""
"What are the advantages and disadvantages with using AI in UX design?"
""
"Advantages:"
"- AI can help UX designers to automate some of the repetitive and time-consuming tasks such as creating wireframes and prototypes, which can save time and increase productivity."
"- AI can analyze user data and behavior patterns to provide insights that can inform the design process and improve user experience."
"- AI can be used to personalize user experiences based on individual preferences and behavior patterns, which can lead to better engagement and increased user satisfaction."
"- AI can help designers to identify and fix potential usability issues before they become problems for users."
""
"Disadvantages:"
"- AI still has limitations in terms of creativity and human intuition, which can be important in designing intuitive and emotionally engaging user experiences."
"- AI algorithms can be biased based on the data they are trained on, which can result in unintentional discrimination or exclusion of certain user groups."
"- AI requires significant investment in terms of infrastructure, training, and maintenance, which can be costly for some organizations."
"- There are ethical considerations around the use of AI in UX design, such as privacy concerns and the potential impact on jobs and human labor."
"#design #ux #ai #chatgpt

Om du lyckas eller inte avgörs mer av dina (och andras) förväntningar än av resultatet. Betyget “Godkänt” på ett prov betyder att du klarat det. Ett lyckat resultat mao. Men hade du sikte inställt på att ha alla rätt så ser du det som ett misslyckande. Samma resultat, olika konsekvenser. "
""
"När vi sätter förväntningar försöker vi styra framtiden i en viss riktning bara med hjälp av tanken. Förväntningar är inga actions, strategi eller planer. De är inte aspirerande som förhoppningar. De är profetior bara sitter där och väntar på att bli uppfyllda."
""
"Ändå kan vi inte låta bli att sätta förväntningar. Vi förväntar oss att en ny iPhone blir bättre än vår gamla, att en ny funktion ska göra produkten bättre, att vårt hårda arbete ska löna sig. Rätt ställda förväntningar driver oss framåt, skapar energi och momentum. "
""
"Det är just det som är kruxet. Rätt förväntningar är konstruktiva, fel ställer till problem och besvikelse. Tyvärr är de förvirrande lika, i alla fall i början. Här är fem råd som hjälper dig på vägen:"
""
"Något kommer att hända, du vet bara inte vad. "
"Resultatet kan bli antingen bra, dåligt eller en blandning. Du kan oftast inte på förhand veta vilket. Du kan däremot: "
"- Hoppas på det bästa "
"- Vara beredd på det värsta"
"- Känna dig nöjd med mitten (det blev iaf inte det värsta)"
""
"Sätta realistiska förväntningar tillsammans"
"Dina kunder eller användare har sina förväntningar på vilken nytta ert samarbete eller produkt ska leverera. Det är du som måste se till att dessa förväntningar uttalas, konkretiseras och synkas. "
""
"Acceptans"
"Påverka det du kan påverka, acceptera resten. Så enkelt på pappret, så svårt i verkligheten. "
""
"Låt inte andras förväntningar styra dig"
"Blev du inte advokat, fotbollsproffs eller hjärnkirurg som din familj förväntade sig. Tråkigt. För dem. Du har ett eget liv att leva."
""
"Nobody expects the Spanish inquisition"
"Låt livet överaska dig. Se en film du aldrig hört talas om, ät mat du aldrig smakat förr, prata med folk utan att fråga var de bor, jobbar med eller kommer från. "
"#expectation #happiness #mindful #satisfaction #energy

Kameran är kanske den viktigaste aspekten när vi köper en ny mobil. Filma och fotografera är en naturlig del av vår vardag, både för att dokumentera och för att kommunicera. Vi har all blivit fotografer tack vare att tekniken tagit bort alla jobbiga moment. Ingen mer framkallning, suddiga bilder eller tidskrävande efterbehandling på datorn. Mobilkameran gör allt automatiskt utan att du ens vet om det, både innan, medan och efter att du tagit bilden. Bilder sorterar sig själva fint i album, taggade och klara. Allt för att du ska slippa det tråkiga. "
""
"Fotografering handlar nu om att berätta historier istället för teknik. En personlig bild är viktigare än den hantverksmässiga kvalitet. När alla bilder är tekniskt korrekta blir de snarlika. Perfektion blir snabbt tråkig. Inte konstigt att det kommer en mot-trend, att ta suddiga och pixliga bilder med en gammal digital kamera. Plötsligt blir ens bilder personliga igen. "
""
"Det må vara en tillfällig trend men den visar att det vi vill kommunicera eller uttrycka varken sitter i tekniken eller den hantverksmässiga skickligheten. Precis som alla blev fotografer med en ny iPhone är nu alla designers, illustratörer skribenter med Midjourney och ChatGPT. Varför slösa tid på en massa kurser på Skillshare när en kan skapa något snyggt direkt med några prompter? "
""
"Den frågan kommer vara svår att besvara den närmaste tiden. Vi behöver hitta en ny balans mellan vad vi människor är bra på och hur vi kan utnyttja kraften i AI på bästa sätt. Vem som gjort vad blir oväsentligt. Förr eller senare kommer vi falla tillbaka till grundfrågan: "
"Hur kan vi berätta en personlig historia som berör? "
"#design #digital #ai #midjourney

"Tänk dig att livet är ett spel, typ monopol. Alla spelare är uppdelade i två lag, lag A (apelsiner) och lag B (bananer). Samma regler och spelplan gäller för båda lagen. Den enda skillnaden är att lag A blivit tilldelad en extra tärning medan lag B får bara köra med en. Lagen vet inte om detta, och ser inte själva tärningskasten. Det de ser är resultatet på brädet, hur långt allas pjäser rör sig och vilka beslut spelarna tar. "
""
"Utan större förvåning går det konsekvent bättre för lag även om det förekommer avvikelser. Vissa Apelsiner slår riktigt dåligt och tar tveksamma beslut samtidigt som en del Bananer har tur och och utnyttjar sina resurser på bästa sätt. Men oavsett hur bra Bananerna spelar så når de inte toppskiktet. Resultatet från bara en tärning räcker helt enkelt inte till. "
""
"Bananerna ör missnöjda och menar att något är fel på spelet. Även om tärnignsslagen är dolda är det inte svårt att se de tydliga skillnaderna. Vissa bananer till och med anklagar apelsinerna för fusk och vill att de ska lämna tillbaka gator och hotell.  "
""
"Apelsinerna svarar med att de visst följer reglerna och att resultatet är en kombination av tur och skicklighet. De pekar på sina lagkamrater som inte har lyckats så bra och sen på de enstaka framgångsrika bananerna. “Se där, vi vinner inte alltid. Och ni kan VISST vinna, ni behöver bara sluta klaga, kämpa hårdare, bli bättre, smartare, modigare och skickligare. "
""
"Båda lagen har följ reglerna och gjort sitt bästa. Vissa haft tur, andra otur, vissa har tagit bra beslut och andre inte. Trots det har resultatet blivit olika där förutsättningar spelade mycket större roll än den individuella prestationen. "
""
"Det enda sättet att lösa problemet är att justera förutsättningarna. Straffa inte apelsinerna, ta inte bort deras hotell eller extratärningen. Ge i stället en till tärning till bananerna. Läget kommer fortfarande vara objektivt orättvist men skillnaderna kommer stadigt att minska. "
""
"Jag vet att jag förenklar ett komplext problem. Samtidigt är jag övertygad om att många (Apelsiner) fortfarande inte är medvetna om dessa “osynliga” skillnaderna. De som inte lyft blicken, valt att blunda eller inte vågat säga ifrån. De behöver vara med för att få till långvariga strukturella förändringar och inte bara quickfixes eller like-kampanjer.

Hela världen är designad för (vita, manliga) medelmåttor. Jag själv befinner mig i mitten av denna medelmåttighet. Jag inte sticker ut på något sätt och kan med lätthet glida genom livet. Alla produkter och tjänster funkar för mig. Jag är inte för smart, för rik, för fattig, för lång eller för kort. Jag är också man vilket hjälper betydligt. Jag lever livet med minsta möjliga motstånd. All the world are belongs to me."

"Det låter som en dröm men denna strömlinjeformade tillvara har gjort mig blind. Jag har svårt att fatta att det som är självklart för mig är svårt eller omöjligt för andra. Jag ser inte medvetet ner på andra, men omedvetet ser jag dem inte alls. Jag har aldrig blivit misshandlad, sextrakasserad, förolämpad, mobbad, förminskad, eller nekad något på grund av min hudfärg, namn, religion, kön eller status. Jag vet om att sådant existerar, men inte i min lilla bubbla. "
""
"Detta är ett stort problem. Om jag sätter igång och designar utifrån mig och mina erfarenheter bygger jag bara för de som är precis som jag, den breda massan (av vita medelålders män). Det värsta är att det funkar rent ekonomiskt och att andra utanför mittfåran har anpassats sig sina behov för att matcha mallen. Det är lätt hänt att en ser detta som framgång, att bolaget hittat sin product market fit, lönsamhet, growth eller kundnöjdhet. Det vi inte fått med är alla andra som står utanför som inte är sköna vita snubbar förblindade av sin egen förträfflighet."

"Empati är inte bara ett ord. Det är en medveten förflyttning. Du vill inte bara veta, du vill förstå och känna. Det är enda sättet att öppna dina ögon för världen utanför den trygga bubblan av medelmåttighet. "

Kort version: Ego är ett dåligt substitut för självförtroende. "
""
"Lång version:"
"Egot är som toma kalorier, de smakar gott, ger mättnadskänsla och en vill bara ha mer. Men egot tar bara up plats utan att ge någon näring tillbaka. Det är få runtomkring som blir inspirerade av ens självupptagenhet. "
""
"Ett ego är inte intresserat av att ge, uppmuntra, lyfta andra eller samarbeta, om det inte direkt gynnar en själv. Ett egos näring är uppmärksamhet och beröm. Jo mer desto bättre. Att dela med sig betyder en förlust och svider in i skinnet."
""
"Ego är ett dåligt substitut för självförtroende. Särskilt i början av ens karriär kan osäkerhet gömmas bakom ett påklistrat ego. Det är ett destruktivt sätt att skydda personens verkliga jag eftersom egot till slut tar över. "
" "
"Samtidigt är egot en opålitlig allierad. Jo större desto mer sårbart. Till slut finns det hot och triggerpunkter överallt. Det krävs mer och mer energi för att försvara de inbillade revirgränserna när alla runt omkring upplevs som hot. "
""
"Istället kan du:"
"- Dela med dig. Av kunskap, beröm, idéer, tid i rampljuset. "
"- Reflektera och be om feedback. Bästa sättet att få självinsikt och jobba bort triggerpunkterna. "
"- Humor och självdistans. Mjuka upp de vassa kanterna på ditt ego och omvandla ditt krävande ego till en stark personlighet. "
"- Bli nybörjare. Lär dig något nytt långt ifrån ditt expertområde. Att vara riktigt usel på något ger perspektiv."
"- Be om ursäkt. Har du (eller ditt ego) orsakat skada, gråt eller sårade känslor finns det inget bättre sätt att både börja bygga förtroendet och riva ner egot. "
""
"Yes you can.  😍 "
""

"Fler insikter från boken Facilitera! av Anna Gullstrand"
"Att läsa denna bok är lite som att kika in i en ny värld. Hade jag läst den för några år sen så skulle den inte alls vara lika kraftfull. Kunskap behöver lika mycket timing som rätt format och innehåll. "
""
"Jag hade sett uppmaningen att minska sitt ego och sina åsikter som tråkigt och kontraproduktivt. Jag ville pitcha, ha starka och övertygande åsikter, vinna argument och blända folk med min personlighet och inspirerande design. Folk (kunder, kollegor) skulle köpa mina ideér eftersom de var mycket bättre än deras. "
"Men det slutade sällan så. Mina idéer blev ifrågasatta, eller ratade. Jag kände mig missförstådd och skyllde på den andra partens designmässiga inkompetens. "
""
"Om jag istället hade ett mer neutralt mindset, likt en facilitator, så hade min ingång varit annorlunda. Istället för mitt ego, mina ideér och min design så är det kunden, användaren och vissa behov som ska uppfyllas med hjälp av design. Jag kan lugnt stå bredvid utan att vara känslomässigt investerad och kunna objektivt diskutera designens egenskaper, vad som funkar och vad som kan förbättras. "
""
"Det finns många likheter mellan facilitering och mindfullness. Att kunna lyssna aktivt, stänga ute sina tankar, att ta in hela rummet, att vara med i nuet. Och det känns riktigt skönt att kunna lämna det jobbiga, uppmärksamhetstörstande, osäkra designer-egot. Jag trivdes nog aldrig med det ändå. "
""
